run_name: NATP Module 7: Weights and Measures — Source-First Dry Run
purpose: The purpose of this unit is to introduce a measuring system for weight, length, and volume used by nursing assistant in the clinical setting.

objectives.objective_title: Identify and describe equipment commonly used by the Nurse Assistant for measuring weight, length, height, and volume
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Identify and describe equipment commonly used by the Nurse Assistant for measuring weight, length, height, and volume
objectives.source_excerpt: : A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test Identify and describe equipment commonly used by the Nurse B. Show examples of B. Correctly identify metric Assistant for measuring weight, length, and volume (see module 8). equipment used to and household A. Volume measuring equipment measure weight, length, measuring equipment and volume and the units of measure 1. Graduated cylinders (metric and household) for each a. Marked with lines and numbers b. Usually marked in ounces and milliliters c. Measuring cups and spoons (household) d. Serving containers B. Length measuring equipment 1. Yardstick 2. Tape measure 3. Standing scale with measuring rod a. Marked in fractions of inches, feet b. Marked in centimeters C. Weight measuring equipment 1. Scales a. Marked in ounces, pounds, grams, and kilograms b. Can be electronic or digital 2. Types of scales a. Standing 1) Used for patient/resident who can stand unassisted and not hold onto scale 2) Patient/resident must be steady on feet b. Wheelchair 1) Used for patient/resident who cannot stand unassisted 2) Weigh unoccupied wheelchair, then weigh patient/resident in wheelchair and subtract wheelchair weight from total weight c. Mechanical lift 1) Cloth sling to hold patient/resident and a hydraulic lift to raise and lower 2) Weigh sling and any linen first, then subtract sling/linens from total weight Module 7- Weights and Measures d. Bed scale 1) Used for patient/resident who is on bed rest or unable to be in a chair or ambulate 2) Weigh any linens first then subtract linens from total weight 3) Patient's/resident's body must not be tou

Procedure for measuring weight on a standing scale Measurement Lab measurements on Activity appropriate facility 1. Prior to beginning procedure, make sure the scale is balanced C. Provide sample trays to forms at zero 2. Place paper towel on platform 3. Help patient/resident step onto center of the scale Module 7- Weights and Measures calculate fluid intake 4. Record measurement using correct abbreviation for unit of classmates, one measurement standing and one simulating contractures B. Procedure for measuring length F. Manual Skills 7.5a- 1. objectives.objective_title: Convert between standard time and military time (24 hour clock) Module 7- Weights and Measures
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Convert between standard time and military time (24 hour clock) Module 7- Weights and Measures
objectives.source_excerpt: Convert between standard time and military time (24-hour clock) A. Systems of telling time 1. Standard a. A.M. = morning, midnight to noon b. P.M. = evening, noon to midnight B. Military (24-hour clock) 1. Four digits 2. No colons 3. Do not use A.M. or P.M. 4. Starts one minute after midnight (0001) and ends at midnight 24 hours later (2400) 5. 5. Noon is 1200 Module 7- Weights and Measures
